meta_title: For Students: client workflow to Solve can't finish projects (Intermediate Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to client workflow and overcome can't finish projects. A Intermediate-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Client Workflow to Solve Can't Finish Projects
content.seo_description: Stop abandoning your code. Master a professional client workflow using Antigravity and Claude Code to ship your student projects with confidence.
content.answer_block: The root cause of unfinished projects is a lack of scope management and defined delivery milestones. By adopting a rigid client workflow, you treat your personal projects as professional deliverables. Use Antigravity for architectural scaffolding and Claude Code to automate the heavy lifting, ensuring every feature is a shippable unit.
content.intro:
You know the feeling: a graveyard of half-baked GitHub repositories, abandoned because the initial excitement hit a wall of complexity. You aren't failing because you lack talent; you are failing because you lack a production-grade framework to bridge the gap between 'code that works' and 'a project that is finished.'

It is time to stop coding like a student and start building like a technical lead. By shifting your mindset toward a 'client-first' workflow, you transform yourself from a hobbyist into an architect. This isn't just about finishing code—it's about learning how to scope, execute, and deliver under the pressure of real-world constraints.

steps.title: Define the Minimum Viable Scope
steps.description: Stop feature-creeping your projects. Define exactly three core deliverables that constitute a 'finished' state.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Senior Project Architect. Analyze my project idea and break it down into 3 non-negotiable features that define a MVP. Create a project roadmap in JSON format.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --init --prompt 'Create a README.md based on the MVP roadmap defined by Antigravity.'

steps.title: Architect the Data Model
steps.description: Before writing logic, define the data structure. A solid schema prevents mid-project refactoring that kills momentum.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a database schema for my project using Prisma/SQL. Include clear relationships and indexing strategies for high performance.
steps.claude_code_command: claude 'Generate the schema.prisma file based on the Antigravity design and run migrations.'

steps.title: Automate Boilerplate with Claude Code
steps.description: Don't waste energy on setup. Use automation to generate the foundation so you can focus on the unique business logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide a directory structure and setup script for a modern Next.js/Node application that follows clean architecture principles.
steps.claude_code_command: claude 'Scaffold the project directory structure and install necessary dependencies based on the Antigravity design.'

steps.title: Implement Feature-Gated Development
steps.description: Build in isolated modules. When one module is done, test it, and move to the next. Never leave a feature in a broken state.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a testing suite for the user authentication module, ensuring edge cases are covered before implementation begins.
steps.claude_code_command: claude 'Implement the authentication module and pass the tests generated by Antigravity.'

steps.title: Final Review & Deployment
steps.description: Treat the final phase as a client handoff. Run a final audit to ensure the project meets all initial requirements.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Lead QA Engineer. Review my codebase and point out any technical debt or security vulnerabilities before I deploy.
steps.claude_code_command: claude 'Refactor code based on the QA review and prepare the project for production deployment.'

pro_tips: Always commit your work at the end of every 90-minute 'sprint' to maintain momentum.
Use Claude Code to document your code as you write it, not after; documentation is part of the feature.
If you find yourself stuck for more than 30 minutes, pivot the feature scope rather than forcing a solution.
Treat your local development environment as a production staging area to catch environment bugs early.
Celebrate the 'MVP' release as a major milestone before adding any 'nice-to-have' features.
content.next_steps: After completing your first MVP using this workflow, deploy it to a live environment (like Vercel or Railway) and share the link in our community showcase to get real feedback.
content.conversion_block: Stop dreaming about your portfolio and start shipping real-world applications that get you hired. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
